creator: gerald@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Mohammad S. Babaei from comment #2)
> OK, let me explain a bit more. This is a fresh install of FreeBSD 12.1
> on a VPS with ZFS filesystem
:
> WITH_CCACHE_BUILD=yes
> CCACHE_DIR=/var/cache/ccache

There's a lot of good information in your follow-up, enough for me to
support a very vague theory I had.

From the original log you shared, it does not appear anything crashes;
rather it appaers some that should be there is missing, the cc1 binary:
 
   xgcc: fatal error: cannot execute 'cc1': execvp: No such file or 
   directory compilation terminated.

The way GCC builds is for stage 1 to built with an existing compiler,
the result then to built stage 2, and the result of that to build stage 3,
so building three times, the last time with a compiler that built itself.

I am wondering whether in your case cc1 was built and copied/installed
already, so the build proceeded, alas that copy had not "arrived" yet,
was not visible yet, when there was an attempt to consume it.  Hence
the failure.

That could happen if storage does not reflect a write to some (other)
clients yet or some parallel building is not synchronized properly.

Maybe there is some configuration in ZFS to look into.  Maybe ccache
exhibits an issue or indeed it is running out of RAM (though that I
would expect to trigger a segmentation fault and abort the build).

Two things to try (together) are disabling parallel build and ccache.
